title: DocMed Type Definitions
content:
This file defines the core type definitions used throughout the DocMed repository.

### Purpose

Provides a standardized set of types for representing documentation entries, versions, files, commit information, dependency graphs, application views, and tabs.

### Key Exports

*   **`ManifestEntry`**: Represents an entry in the documentation manifest.
    *   `filePath`: The path to the documentation file.
    *   `title`: The human-readable title of the documentation.
    *   `docKey`: A unique identifier for the documentation entry.
*   **`DocVersion`**: Represents a specific version of a documentation file.
    *   `timestamp`: The timestamp of the version.
    *   `title`: The title of the documentation at this version.
    *   `content`: The markdown content of the documentation.
    *   `dependencies`: An array of document keys that this version depends on.
    *   `commitId`: The commit ID associated with this version (or null).
*   **`DocFile`**: Represents a complete documentation file, potentially with multiple versions.
    *   `filePath`: The path to the documentation file.
    *   `title`: The main title of the documentation file.
    *   `content`: The current content of the documentation file.
    *   `dependencies`: Optional array of document keys this file depends on.
    *   `versions`: Optional array of historical `DocVersion` objects.
*   **`Commit`**: Represents commit information.
    *   `id`: The commit hash.
    *   `message`: The commit message.
    *   `pusher`: The name of the committer.
    *   `timestamp`: The commit timestamp.
*   **`DependencyGraph`**: A record mapping a document key to an array of document keys it depends on.
*   **`View`**: Represents different possible views within the DocMed application.
    *   `home`: The home page.
    *   `commits`: A view listing commits.
    *   `doc`: A view displaying a specific document, identified by `key`.
    *   `folder`: A view displaying the contents of a folder, identified by `path`.
*   **`Tab`**: Represents a tab in the UI.
    *   `id`: A unique identifier for the tab.
    *   `view`: The `View` associated with this tab.
    *   `label`: The text label for the tab.

### Usage Example

```typescript
// Example of creating a DocFile
const myDoc: DocFile = {
  filePath: 'docs/guide.md',
  title: 'Getting Started Guide',
  content: '# Welcome to DocMed!',
  dependencies: ['introduction'],
  versions: [
    {
      timestamp: '2023-10-27T10:00:00Z',
      title: 'Initial Draft',
      content: '# Welcome to DocMed!\n\nThis is the first version.',
      dependencies: [],
      commitId: 'abcdef123456'
    }
  ]
};

// Example of a Tab for the home view
const homeTab: Tab = {
  id: 'home-tab',
  view: { type: 'home' },
  label: 'Home'
};
```

versions.title: DocMed Types
versions.content:
This file defines the core type definitions used throughout the DocMed repository.

## Key Exports

- `ManifestEntry`: Represents an entry in the documentation manifest, including its file path, display title, and a unique document key.
- `DocVersion`: Defines the structure for a specific version of a document, including its timestamp, title, content, dependencies, and the Git commit ID.
- `DocFile`: Represents a single documentation file, containing its path, title, content, optional dependencies, and an array of its historical versions.
- `Commit`: Represents a Git commit, storing its ID, message, pusher, and timestamp.
- `DependencyGraph`: A type alias for a record where keys are document keys and values are arrays of document keys that the key document depends on.
- `View`: A discriminated union type representing different possible views within the DocMed application (e.g., 'home', 'commits', 'deps', 'doc', 'folder').
- `Tab`: Represents a tab in the UI, containing a unique ID, the associated `View`, and a user-facing label.

## Usage Example

```typescript
import type { DocFile, View } from "./types";

const exampleDoc: DocFile = {
  filePath: "src/docs/introduction.md",
  title: "Introduction to DocMed",
  content: "# Welcome to DocMed!\nThis is the introduction.",
  dependencies: ["getting-started"],
  versions: [
    {
      timestamp: "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
      title: "Initial Draft",
      content: "# Welcome!\nFirst version.",
      dependencies: [],
      commitId: "abcdef123",
    },
  ],
};

const currentView: View = { type: "doc", key: "introduction" };
```

DocMed Type Definitions
versions.content:
This file defines the core type definitions used throughout the DocMed repository.

### Purpose

Provides a standardized set of types for representing documentation entries, versions, files, commit information, dependency graphs, application views, and tabs.

### Key Exports

*   **`ManifestEntry`**: Represents an entry in the documentation manifest.
    *   `filePath`: The path to the documentation file.
    *   `title`: The human-readable title of the documentation.
    *   `docKey`: A unique identifier for the documentation entry.
*   **`DocVersion`**: Represents a specific version of a documentation file.
    *   `timestamp`: The timestamp of the version.
    *   `title`: The title of the documentation at this version.
    *   `content`: The markdown content of the documentation.
    *   `dependencies`: An array of document keys that this version depends on.
    *   `commitId`: The commit ID associated with this version (or null).
*   **`DocFile`**: Represents a complete documentation file, potentially with multiple versions.
    *   `filePath`: The path to the documentation file.
    *   `title`: The main title of the documentation file.
    *   `content`: The current content of the documentation file.
    *   `dependencies`: Optional array of document keys this file depends on.
    *   `versions`: Optional array of historical `DocVersion` objects.
*   **`Commit`**: Represents commit information.
    *   `id`: The commit hash.
    *   `message`: The commit message.
    *   `pusher`: The name of the committer.
    *   `timestamp`: The commit timestamp.
*   **`DependencyGraph`**: A record mapping a document key to an array of document keys it depends on.
*   **`View`**: Represents different possible views within the DocMed application.
    *   `home`: The home page.
    *   `commits`: A view listing commits.
    *   `doc`: A view displaying a specific document, identified by `key`.
    *   `folder`: A view displaying the contents of a folder, identified by `path`.
*   **`Tab`**: Represents a tab in the UI.
    *   `id`: A unique identifier for the tab.
    *   `view`: The `View` associated with this tab.
    *   `label`: The text label for the tab.

### Usage Example

```typescript
// Example of creating a DocFile
const myDoc: DocFile = {
  filePath: 'docs/guide.md',
  title: 'Getting Started Guide',
  content: '# Welcome to DocMed!',
  dependencies: ['introduction'],
  versions: [
    {
      timestamp: '2023-10-27T10:00:00Z',
      title: 'Initial Draft',
      content: '# Welcome to DocMed!\n\nThis is the first version.',
      dependencies: [],
      commitId: 'abcdef123456'
    }
  ]
};

// Example of a Tab for the home view
const homeTab: Tab = {
  id: 'home-tab',
  view: { type: 'home' },
  label: 'Home'
};
```
